channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Woman in Tanzania Gives Birth to Quadruplets Despite Expecting Triplets
item.description: A woman identified as Rachael Simba and her husband Paschal experienced an unexpected turn of events after the birth of their children at Kilimanjaro Christian Medical Centre. The couple had prepared ...

item.title: Delhi Kailash Hills rape-murder case: Accused checked into hotel after crime, say police
item.description: A hotel manager in Delhi’s Dwarka area has recounted his interaction with Rahul Meena, the 23-year-old accused in the brutal rape and murder of a 22-year-old woman in Kailash Hills. According to the m...

item.title: US and Iran remain in deadlock as indirect talks resume in Pakistan amid ongoing conflict
item.description: Nakatakdang bumiyahe ang mga negosyador ng Estados Unidos papuntang Islamabad, Pakistan para sa panibagong round ng usapang pangkapayapaan, pero nilinaw ng Iran na hindi ito makikipagharap nang direkt...

item.title: Vice Ganda's Game Causes a Stir on It's Showtime
item.description: During a recent episode of ABS-CBN’s ‘It’s Showtime,’ a playful interaction between Vice Ganda and Ogie Alcasid sparked a lighthearted but noticeable reaction. Apparently, Vice Ganda incorporated a ga...

item.title: Orengo Rejects Luo Elders' Mediation Amid ODM Rift
item.description: On April 25, Kenyan newspapers highlighted ongoing political tensions in Nyanza, particularly within the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) party. The Taifa Leo reported that the Luo Council of Elders t...

item.title: Gazans Participate in Local Elections for the First Time in Two Decades
item.description: On April 25, 2026, Palestinians in Gaza were able to cast their votes in local elections for the first time in over twenty years, marking a significant event in the political landscape of the region. ...

item.title: Falklands Veteran Urges King to Address US Position During Visit
item.description: The article reports on Falklands War veteran Simon Weston’s reaction to reports suggesting that the United States might reconsider its stance on the United Kingdom’s sovereignty over the Falkland Isla...

item.title: Challenges and Solutions for Affordable Housing in Pittsburgh
item.description: Pittsburgh is often seen as an affordable city due to lower-than-average living costs and stable employment. However, a 2026 survey reveals that single adults need an annual income of $95,000, well ab...

item.title: Brazilian influencer Tamyris Teixeira Santos found dead days after going missing during beach swim
item.description: A Brazilian content creator, lawyer, and martial arts enthusiast, Tamyris Teixeira Santos, was found dead on a beach in Rio de Janeiro three days after she went missing. The 36-year-old influencer had...

item.title: UBA Sees 9.4% Growth in Assets, Focuses on Long-Term Sustainability
item.description: United Bank for Africa (UBA) has announced a 9.4% increase in its total assets, now valued at N33.2 trillion, compared to N30.3 trillion in 2024. This growth is attributed to a strategic repositioning...

item.title: Man extradited from UAE faces murder charge over 2024 stabbing in Westminster
item.description: A man has been extradited from the United Arab Emirates and charged with the murder of a woman following a stabbing incident in Westminster, central London, in 2024. The victim, identified as Kamonnan...

item.title: Retired Navy official Trinidad to continue as AFP spokesperson for West Philippine Sea
item.description: Rear Admiral Roy Vincent Trinidad will continue serving as the Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) spokesperson for the West Philippine Sea (WPS) even after officially retiring from active military ...

item.title: Repeated global energy shocks drive shift towards diversified and local energy systems
item.description: Over the past decade, the world has experienced an unusual cluster of major energy crises, reshaping how countries think about energy security and supply. Beginning with the post-pandemic inflation su...

item.title: Russian Oligarch's Daughters Lose Appeal Against Canada's Sanctions
item.description: Laura and Katia Fridman, daughters of Russian oligarch Mikhail Fridman, have failed in their court appeal to be removed from Canada's sanctions list. The sanctions were imposed in response to Russia's...

item.title: Court Revokes Rajab Butt's Bail Due to Absence in Religious Sentiment Case
item.description: In a recent development, a Karachi sessions court has canceled the interim bail granted to YouTuber Rajab Butt due to his failure to appear in court for the confirmation of his bail. The case revolves...

item.title: States and DisCos face major challenges in Nigeria's electricity sector reform
item.description: Nigeria’s power sector reform is reaching a critical phase as states begin taking greater control under the Electricity Act of 2023. The PwC report highlights a transition towards a decentralised elec...

item.title: Calgary Fire Crews Deal with Wind-Related Emergencies as Gusts Reach 90 km/h
item.description: The Calgary Fire Department has been responding to a surge of wind-related incidents as powerful gusts of up to 90 km/h battered the city. Over the past 24 hours, crews have attended more than 225 eme...

item.title: Daily horoscope insights for each zodiac sign on April 25, 2026
item.description: This horoscope column by Sally Brompton offers guidance for each zodiac sign, with an overarching theme of caution, self-awareness, and emotional control influenced by strong planetary alignments, par...

item.title: Celestine Ndinda Addresses Breakup After Leaked Chats Reveal Reasons
item.description: Veteran Kenyan comedian Njugush and his wife Celestine Ndinda, popularly known as Wakavinye, have publicly confirmed their separation after 14 years of marriage. The split, which occurred in 2025, was...

item.title: South Africa reflects on 32 years of democracy as unemployment and inequality persist
item.description: South Africa marks 32 years since the end of apartheid and the beginning of its constitutional democracy, a period defined by major political and social gains alongside persistent economic challenges....
